ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of the weather data measurement */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the location where the weather data is collected */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season representation (1 for winter, 2 for spring, 3 for summer, 4 for autumn) */,
  "holiday" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the day is a public holiday (1 for holiday, 0 for non-holiday) */,
  "workingday" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the day is a working day (1 for working day, 0 for weekend/day off) */,
  "weather" INTEGER /* Weather condition categorical integer code */,
  "temp" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius */,
  "atemp" REAL /* Apparent temperature considering humidity and wind chill */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Relative humidity percentage */,
  "windspeed" REAL /* Wind speed in meters per second */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Amount of precipitation in millimeters */,
  "snow_depth" REAL /* Depth of snow in centimeters */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Visibility in kilometers */,
  "cloud_cover" INTEGER /* Percentage of cloud cover */,
  "aqi" INTEGER /* Air Quality Index value */,
  "pm2_5" REAL /* Concentration of PM2.5 particles in micrograms per cubic meter */,
  "pm10" REAL /* Concentration of PM10 particles in micrograms per cubic meter */,
  "traffic_volume" INTEGER /* Traffic volume in the area */,
  "energy_consumption" REAL /* Energy consumption in the area */,
  "public_events" INTEGER /* Indicator of any public events in the area (1 for event, 0 for no event) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the weather data */,
  "data_quality" INTEGER /* Quality of the data, ranging from 0 (poor) to 100 (excellent) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("datetime", "location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_weather FOREIGN KEY ("weather") REFERENCES weather_conditions ("weather_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "weather_code" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the weather condition (e.g., 1 for mild, 2 for moderate, 3 for severe) */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Possible impact of the weather condition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_code")
)
column_names: Timestamp of the weather data measurement
Unique identifier for the location where the weather data is collected
